what: New R-based User-Friendly Spatial Metabolomic, Transcriptomic, and Proteomic Data Analysis Tool
title: User Friendly Spatial Metabolomic Data Analysis Package
text: SpaMTP R based wrapper package Seurat analysis spatial metabolomic data. user-freindly package contains various function analysis, integration visalisation multi-modal datasets, particularly focusing metabolomic/transcriptomics integration. package includes various functions preform pre-processing, spatial visualisation, various -stream biological centered analyses, data integration data export Spatial Metabolomic data. addition, package ability use Cardinal (Spatial Metabolomic based software) Seurat (Spatial Transcriptomic based software) functions.

title: Combines rigid tranformation matrices — combine.tr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(imager) library(tidyverse) im <- load.image("https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fd/Aster_Tataricus.JPG/1024px-Aster_Tataricus.JPG") d <- sRGBtoLab(im) %>% as.data.frame(wide="c")%>%   dplyr::select(-x,-y)  km <- kmeans(d, 2)  # Run a segmentation to extract flower seg <- as.cimg(abs(km$cluster - 2), dim = c(dim(im)[1:2], 1, 1)) plot(seg); highlight(seg == 1)  # Detect edges dx <- imgradient(seg, "x") dy <- imgradient(seg, "y") grad.mag <- sqrt(dx^2 + dy^2) plot(grad.mag)  # Extract points at edges edges.px <- which(grad.mag > max(grad.mag[, , 1, 1])/2, arr.ind = TRUE) points(edges.px, col = "green", cex = 0.1)  # Apply transformations to point set tr1 <- combine.tr(center.cur = apply(edges.px[, 1:2], 2, mean),                   center.new = c(1200, 1200), alpha = 90) tr2 <- combine.tr(center.cur = apply(edges.px[, 1:2], 2, mean),                   center.new = c(500, 1200), mirror.x = T, alpha = 30) tr3 <- combine.tr(center.cur = apply(edges.px[, 1:2], 2, mean),                   center.new = c(1200, 500), mirror.y = T, alpha = 270) plot(edges.px, xlim = c(0, 1700), ylim = c(0, 1700), cex = 0.1) points(t(tr1%*%t(edges.px[, 1:3])), cex = 0.1, col = "red") points(t(tr2%*%t(edges.px[, 1:3])), cex = 0.1, col = "yellow") points(t(tr3%*%t(edges.px[, 1:3])), cex = 0.1, col = "blue") } # }
